title: Search Optimization: Fuzzy Search Cache

tasks.description: Define FuzzyCacheKey Interface - Create interface with query, threshold, limit, and offset fields for generating unique cache keys
acceptanceCriteria: FuzzyCacheKey interface defined with query, threshold/maxDistance, limit, offset
Query field stored in lowercase for normalization
Interface supports cache key comparison

tasks.description: Implement Fuzzy Search Result Cache - Use SearchCache class from utils/searchCache.ts (new SearchCache<KnowledgeGraph>(100, 300000)), add private cache property with maxSize 100 and TTL 5 minutes, implement cache lookup before computation in fuzzySearch()
testCategories: Cache miss computes and stores results
Different query params create different cache keys
acceptanceCriteria: Import SearchCache class from ../utils/searchCache.js
Private cache property initialized with maxSize: 100, ttlMs: 300000 (5 min)
fuzzySearch() checks cache before computing
Cache key generated from normalized query + threshold + limit + offset
Results cached after computation
Cache returns same object reference (no deep copy)

tasks.description: Add Cache Invalidation Method - Implement invalidateCache() method that clears the fuzzy search cache, hook into storage change notifications for automatic invalidation on entity mutations
acceptanceCriteria: invalidateCache() method implemented in FuzzySearch
Method clears entire cache
SearchManager can call invalidateCache() after mutations
Cache entries expire after TTL (5 minutes)
LRU eviction when cache exceeds maxSize

tasks.description: Add Fuzzy Search Cache Tests - Test cache hit/miss behavior, performance improvement, cache invalidation, TTL expiration, and LRU eviction
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies cache hit returns identical object
Test verifies first query takes full computation time
Test verifies second identical query is <1ms
Test verifies different threshold creates new cache entry
Test verifies invalidateCache() clears all entries
Test verifies TTL-based expiration
12+ new tests added

successCriteria: Fuzzy search cache implemented with 100-entry limit
Cache properly invalidated on entity mutations
All existing fuzzy search tests pass
notes: Fuzzy search is expensive due to Levenshtein distance calculations on every entity. Caching dramatically improves repeated query performance. The cache uses both TTL-based expiration and entity mutation invalidation to ensure freshness. This pattern will be reused for Boolean search caching in Sprint 4. This sprint is independent of Sprints 1-2 (storage optimizations) and can be developed in parallel.
